items.situation: Two students discuss a new downtown restaurant and which dish to try.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new Korean place on Fifth Street yet? I keep hearing about it, but I can't decide if it's worth the trip.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, I went last week! Honestly, the dumplings everyone raves about were just okay. A little greasy, actually.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? That's what I was planning to order.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Well, don't waste it on the dumplings. The spicy tofu stew is incredible. It's not even on the main menu, though. You have to ask the server for it.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Huh, a secret dish. Okay, I'm intrigued now.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Trust me, it's the only reason I'd go back. Just remember to ask.

questions.stem: What are the two students mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether a new restaurant is worth visiting and what to order
options.B: How to get a job at a downtown restaurant
options.C: A cooking class they both want to take
options.D: Why the restaurant raised its prices recently

questions.explanation: 对话围绕一家新开的韩国餐厅是否值得去以及点什么菜展开，女士在犹豫、男士给出建议，所以A正确。B（找餐厅工作）、C（烹饪课）都未被提及，属于wrong_topic。D（涨价）虽是餐厅相关话题，但对话从未谈到价格，属于too_narrow式的偏离主旨。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do when she goes to the restaurant?

options.B: Avoid the restaurant after hearing his review
options.C: Ask the server about the spicy tofu stew
options.D: Complain to the manager about greasy food

questions.explanation: 男士强调豆腐汤是隐藏菜单需主动向服务员询问，女士说"我现在有兴趣了"，可推断她会去问豆腐汤，所以C正确。A是男士明确劝阻的选项（rejected_option）。B与女士表现出的兴趣相反，且她仍想去（rejected_option）。D投诉经理在对话中毫无依据且过度发挥（over_inference）。

items.situation: Two students chat about a part-time job one just started at a factory.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: So how's the new job at the bottling factory going? You started this week, right?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, three shifts so far. The work itself is fine, but the schedule is brutal. They have me on the six a.m. start.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Six a.m.? Yikes. How do you even get there that early?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's the problem. The first campus bus doesn't run until seven, so I've been biking forty minutes in the dark.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That sounds exhausting. Can't you ask for a later shift?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm going to talk to my supervisor on Friday. If she can't move me, I honestly don't know how long I'll last.

questions.stem: Why does the man say the schedule is difficult for him?

options.A: The factory is too far from the bus stop
options.B: His shift starts before the campus bus runs
options.C: He has to work three shifts every day
options.D: His classes overlap with his work hours

questions.explanation: 男士说班次早上六点开始，但第一班校车七点才发，所以他得骑车，B正确。A把问题说成工厂离站点远，是改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。C"每天三个班次"误读了"目前共上了三个班"（wrong_detail）。D课程与工作冲突在对话中从未提及（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What does the man imply he might do if his shift is not changed?

options.A: Buy a faster bicycle for the commute
options.B: Move into an apartment near the factory
options.C: Report the factory to the university

questions.explanation: 男士说如果主管不能调班，他不知道自己还能坚持多久，暗示他可能辞职，所以D正确。A买更快的自行车在对话中未提及（unrelated_next_step）。B搬到工厂附近也未被讨论（unrelated_next_step）。C向学校举报工厂属于无依据的过度发挥（over_inference）。

items.situation: Two students talk about a houseplant one is caring for.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, can you do me a favor? I'm visiting my parents this weekend, and I need someone to water my fern.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sure, no problem. How often does it need water?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Just once, on Saturday. But please don't overdo it. The soil should be damp, not soaked.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Got it. Damp, not soaked. Should I put it by the window?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Actually, no. Keep it out of direct sunlight, or the leaves will turn brown. The corner of my desk is perfect.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Okay, I'll leave it right where it is then. Have a great trip!

questions.stem: What does the woman ask the man to do?

options.A: Move the fern next to the window
options.B: Water the plant every day she is away
options.C: Water her fern once on Saturday
options.D: Buy a new pot for the fern

questions.explanation: 女士明确说周六只浇一次水，所以C正确。A把植物移到窗边正是她叮嘱不要做的（misattributed/reversed）。B每天浇水与她说的"只浇一次"矛盾（wrong_detail）。D买新花盆从未被提及（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: Where will the man most likely keep the fern?

options.A: On the corner of the woman's desk
options.B: On the windowsill in the sunlight
options.D: In a dark closet for the weekend

questions.explanation: 女士说桌角最合适，男士回答"我就把它放在原处"，可推断他会把蕨类留在桌角，所以A正确。B放在阳光下的窗台正是女士警告会让叶子变黄的做法（rejected_option）。C放到阳台、D放进黑暗壁橱都未被提及且与对话无关（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: Two students compare how they get to campus — bike, bus, or walk.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm so tired of the campus shuttle. It's always packed and it's never on time. How do you get here?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I used to take the bus too, but I switched to biking last month. It's actually faster door to door.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Really? Doesn't it get exhausting, though?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not really. It's only fifteen minutes, and there's a protected bike lane now. Plus I save twenty dollars a month on the bus pass.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Twenty dollars? That adds up. But I don't even own a bike.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: The student union rents them by the semester for almost nothing. You should check it out before you commit to another bus pass.

questions.stem: According to the man, what is one advantage of biking over taking the bus?

options.A: The bike lane is shaded and scenic
options.B: Biking gives him more exercise each day
options.C: He can leave home much later in the morning
options.D: He saves twenty dollars a month

questions.explanation: 男士明确说骑车每月省下二十美元的公交月票费用，所以D正确。A说车道有遮荫、风景好，对话只提到有受保护的车道，改了关键事实（wrong_detail）。B多锻炼身体从未被提及（related_but_not_stated）。C可以晚出门也未被讨论（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do next?

options.A: Buy a brand-new bicycle from a shop
options.B: Look into renting a bike from the student union
options.C: Continue using the campus shuttle as before
options.D: Ask the man to give her rides to campus

questions.explanation: 女士说自己没有自行车，男士建议去学生会租车且很便宜，可推断她会去了解租车，所以B正确。A买全新自行车与对话中"便宜租用"的建议不符，也未被考虑（unrelated_next_step）。C继续坐班车与她抱怨班车又挤又晚相矛盾（rejected_option）。D让男士载她从未被提及（unrelated_next_step）。

items.situation: Two students compare notes on a novel they both read.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Did you finish the novel for Professor Lin's class? The one about the lighthouse keeper?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did! I loved it, but honestly the ending confused me. I had to read the last chapter twice.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Same here. The whole thing about the missing letters never really got explained.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Right? I'm hoping Professor Lin clears that up in the lecture tomorrow.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Actually, I was going to email her tonight to ask. Want me to send you what she says?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That'd be great, thanks. I'll just wait to hear back from you before the lecture.

questions.stem: What did both students find confusing about the novel?

options.A: The ending and the missing letters
options.B: The name of the lighthouse keeper
options.C: The historical setting of the story
options.D: Why the professor assigned the book

questions.explanation: 两人都提到结尾让人困惑、关于失踪信件的情节没有交代，所以A正确。B灯塔看守人的名字从未被讨论（wrong_detail）。C故事的历史背景未被提及（related_but_not_stated）。D教授为何布置此书也未被谈到（related_but_not_stated）。

questions.stem: What will the woman most likely do tonight?

options.A: Reread the last chapter of the novel
options.B: Wait until the lecture to ask her question
options.C: Email the professor about the ending
options.D: Start reading the next assigned book

questions.explanation: 女士说今晚打算给林教授发邮件询问，可推断她会发邮件，所以C正确。A重读最后一章是男士做过的事，被误植到女士身上（misattributed）。B等到讲座再问与她主动发邮件的计划相反（rejected_option）。D开始读下一本书在对话中毫无依据（unrelated_next_step）。
